title: What's Really Inside AI?

modules.title: Meet the Machine That Guesses
modules.sub: Discover why AI predicts instead of truly knowing
modules.description: Learners step into the role of junior engineer and get their first look at how an AI system makes decisions by guessing from patterns. They run a simple prediction experiment to see firsthand why the machine is confident but not always correct.

modules.title: Training Day: Teaching with Data
modules.sub: See how data shapes everything an AI learns
modules.description: Learners explore how an AI model is built by feeding it examples, discovering that the quality and variety of training data directly controls what the AI can and cannot do. They sort through a messy dataset and decide which examples are helpful or harmful to include.

modules.title: Pattern Party: How AI Learns
modules.sub: Uncover the hidden patterns AI uses to decide
modules.description: Learners investigate how AI detects repeating patterns across thousands of examples to form its internal rules. They play a pattern-matching challenge that mirrors what a real model does, then reflect on which patterns are useful and which are misleading.

modules.title: When AI Gets It Totally Wrong
modules.sub: Diagnose the glitches causing chaos at headquarters
modules.description: Learners examine a series of real-style AI failures inside the story and work out why each one happened, linking errors back to problems in training data or flawed patterns. They begin drafting their AI error report by logging each glitch with a short explanation.

modules.title: You Are the Trainer Now
modules.sub: Take control and train an AI your own way
modules.description: Learners step into the trainer's seat and make deliberate choices about which data to use, adjusting inputs to steer the AI toward better or worse outcomes. They observe how their decisions change the model's behaviour and add findings to their growing error report.

modules.title: Design Your Own AI Rule
modules.sub: Write the rules that keep an AI fair and accurate
modules.description: Learners synthesise everything they have learned by designing a clear, testable rule that an AI system should follow to avoid a specific type of mistake. They complete and present their finished AI error report, explaining the failure, its cause, and their proposed fix.

synopsis: You've been hired as a junior engineer at a company whose AI keeps making strange mistakes â and it's your job to figure out why. By investigating each glitch inside the story, you'll uncover exactly how AI models are trained, how they reason, and where they fall apart.
rationale: Students aged 11-13 interact with AI tools daily but overwhelmingly treat them as magic, leaving them unable to critically evaluate outputs or understand why errors occur.
learning_outcome: Students can explain in their own words how an AI model learns from data, predict the kinds of mistakes it will make, and produce a written 'AI error report' diagnosing a real failure.
